Basic_NT board 70: Lead the ♠10 — top of nothing in your five-card spade suit. The ♥A-K tempt, but vs notrump length beats quick tricks; keep them as entries.
Basic_NT board 42: Lead the ♥7 — fourth best from ♥A-Q-9-7-6-3. Lead low, not the ace: keep the tenace working over declarer.
Basic_NT board 94: Lead the ♥7 — fourth best from ♥A-9-8-7-5-2. Keep the ace as stopper and entry; lead low to build your length.
Basic_NT board 455: Lead the ♦Q — top of the interior run in ♦A-Q-J-8-7-6. The queen drives out the king while the ace stays poised over declarer.
Basic_NT board 319: Lead the ♥10 — top of nothing in your six-card heart suit. The ♣J-10-9 tempts, but three clubs can't match six hearts.
Basic_NT board 162: Lead the ♥10 — top of nothing in your six-card heart suit. Length over the scattered ♣K-J; the ♠A is your entry, not a lead.

Basic_NT board 367: Lead the ♥K — top of your ♥K-Q-10. Drive out the ace and build your five-card suit; the scattered queens are no place to lead from.
Basic_NT board 218: Lead the ♥4 — fourth best from your six-card heart suit. The ♠A and ♦K are entries, not leads; attack your length.

Basic_NT board 200: Lead the ♠J — top of your ♠J-10-9. A six-card sequence is exactly what beats notrump: lead from the top.
Basic_NT board 55: Lead the ♠9 — top of nothing in your five-card spade suit. The ♣A is your entry, not a lead; length beats the stray ♦Q.
Basic_NT board 378: Lead the ♠10 — top of the interior run in ♠Q-10-9-7-3. The ♥J-10-9 is pretty, but three cards can't outrun five.

Basic_NT board 13: Lead the ♠Q — top of your ♠Q-J-10. The top of a solid sequence can't cost and promises the cards beneath; keep leading spades.
Basic_NT board 110: Lead the ♦J — top of your ♦J-10-9. Attack your safe sequence; leading from the ♠Q or ♣K would risk a trick.
Basic_NT board 68: Lead the ♠J — top of your ♠J-10-8. The red aces are entries to bring the spades in, not leads.
Basic_NT board 189: Lead the ♠7 — fourth best from ♠Q-9-8-7-5-2. Lead low, not the queen; the ♦A is your entry.
Basic_NT board 187: Lead the ♠6 — fourth best from ♠Q-8-7-6-3-2. Lead low, not the queen, and build your length; the ♥K is a likely entry.
Basic_NT board 153: Lead the ♠K — top of your ♠K-Q-J. Three touching honours, safe from the top; no longer suit to prefer.
Basic_NT board 41: Lead the ♣7 — top of nothing in your five-card club suit. The ♥A is your entry, not a lead; length beats the scattered ♠Q.
Basic_NT board 238: Lead the ♥J — top of the interior run in ♥K-J-10-9-5-3. It drives out a stopper while the king waits above declarer.
Basic_NT board 202: Lead the ♥K — top of your ♥K-Q-10. Drive out the ace and your six-card suit comes alive.
Basic_NT board 137: Lead the ♠10 — top of nothing in your long spade suit. The ♦A is your sure entry, not a lead; let length do the work.
